root_text.sn56.11:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā bārāṇasiyaṁ viharati isipatane migadāye.
root_text.sn56.11:1.2: Tatra kho bhagavā pañcavaggiye bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn56.11:2.3: Yo cāyaṁ kāmesu kāmasukhallikānuyogo hīno gammo pothujjaniko anariyo anatthasaṁhito, yo cāyaṁ attakilamathānuyogo dukkho anariyo anatthasaṁhito.
root_text.sn56.11:2.4: Ete kho, bhikkhave, ubho ante anupagamma majjhimā paṭipadā tathāgatena abhisambuddhā cakkhukaraṇī ñāṇakaraṇī upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn56.11:3.1: Katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, majjhimā paṭipadā tathāgatena abhisambuddhā cakkhukaraṇī ñāṇakaraṇī upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattati?
root_text.sn56.11:3.3: sammādiṭṭhi sammāsaṅkappo sammāvācā sammākammanto sammāājīvo sammāvāyāmo sammāsati sammāsamādhi.
root_text.sn56.11:3.4: Ayaṁ kho sā, bhikkhave, majjhimā paṭipadā tathāgatena abhisambuddhā cakkhukaraṇī ñāṇakaraṇī upasamāya abhiññāya sambodhāya nibbānāya saṁvattati.
root_text.sn56.11:4.1: Idaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ—
root_text.sn56.11:4.2: jātipi dukkhā, jarāpi dukkhā, byādhipi dukkho, maraṇampi dukkhaṁ, appiyehi sampayogo dukkho, piyehi vippayogo dukkho, yampicchaṁ na labhati tampi dukkhaṁ—saṅkhittena pañcupādānakkhandhā dukkhā.
root_text.sn56.11:4.3: Idaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ—
root_text.sn56.11:4.4: yāyaṁ taṇhā ponobbhavikā nandirāgasahagatā tatratatrābhinandinī, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn56.11:4.6: Idaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ—
root_text.sn56.11:4.7: yo tassāyeva taṇhāya asesavirāganirodho cāgo paṭinissaggo mutti anālayo.
root_text.sn56.11:4.8: Idaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā ariyasaccaṁ—
root_text.sn56.11:5.1: ‘Idaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:5.2: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ pariññeyyan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe …pe… udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:5.3: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ pariññātan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:6.1: ‘Idaṁ dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:6.2: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ pahātabban’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe …pe… udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:6.3: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ pahīnan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:7.1: ‘Idaṁ dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:7.2: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ sacchikātabban’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe …pe… udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:7.3: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ sacchikatan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:8.1: ‘Idaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā ariyasaccan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:8.2: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā ariyasaccaṁ bhāvetabban’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe …pe… udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:8.3: ‘Taṁ kho panidaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā ariyasaccaṁ bhāvitan’ti me, bhikkhave, pubbe ananussutesu dhammesu cakkhuṁ udapādi, ñāṇaṁ udapādi, paññā udapādi, vijjā udapādi, āloko udapādi.
root_text.sn56.11:9.1: Yāvakīvañca me, bhikkhave, imesu catūsu ariyasaccesu evaṁ tiparivaṭṭaṁ dvādasākāraṁ yathābhūtaṁ ñāṇadassanaṁ na suvisuddhaṁ ahosi, neva tāvāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.sn56.11:10.1: Yato ca kho me, bhikkhave, imesu catūsu ariyasaccesu evaṁ tiparivaṭṭaṁ dvādasākāraṁ yathābhūtaṁ ñāṇadassanaṁ suvisuddhaṁ ahosi, athāhaṁ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ‘anuttaraṁ sammāsambodhiṁ abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ.
root_text.sn56.11:10.3: ‘akuppā me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.
root_text.sn56.11:10.5: Attamanā pañcavaggiyā bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.
root_text.sn56.11:11.1: Imasmiñca pana veyyākaraṇasmiṁ bhaññamāne āyasmato koṇḍaññassa virajaṁ vītamalaṁ dhammacakkhuṁ udapādi:
root_text.sn56.11:12.1: Pavattite ca pana bhagavatā dhammacakke bhummā devā saddamanussāvesuṁ:
root_text.sn56.11:12.2: “etaṁ bhagavatā bārāṇasiyaṁ isipatane migadāye anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavattitaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmin”ti.
root_text.sn56.11:12.3: Bhummānaṁ devānaṁ saddaṁ sutvā cātumahārājikā devā saddamanussāvesuṁ:
root_text.sn56.11:12.4: “etaṁ bhagavatā bārāṇasiyaṁ isipatane migadāye anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavattitaṁ, appaṭivattiyaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmin”ti.
root_text.sn56.11:12.5: Cātumahārājikānaṁ devānaṁ saddaṁ sutvā tāvatiṁsā devā …pe…
root_text.sn56.11:12.11: “etaṁ bhagavatā bārāṇasiyaṁ isipatane migadāye anuttaraṁ dhammacakkaṁ pavattitaṁ appaṭivattiyaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmin”ti.
root_text.sn56.11:13.1: Itiha tena khaṇena tena layena tena muhuttena yāva brahmalokā saddo abbhuggacchi.
root_text.sn56.11:13.2: Ayañca dasasahassilokadhātu saṅkampi sampakampi sampavedhi, appamāṇo ca uḷāro obhāso loke pāturahosi atikkamma devānaṁ devānubhāvanti.
root_text.sn56.11:14.1: Atha kho bhagavā imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.sn56.11:14.2: “aññāsi vata bho, koṇḍañño, aññāsi vata bho, koṇḍañño”ti.
root_text.sn56.11:14.3: Iti hidaṁ āyasmato koṇḍaññassa “aññāsikoṇḍañño” tveva nāmaṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn56.11:0.2: 2. Rolling Forth the Wheel of Dhamma
translation_text.sn56.11:0.3: Rolling Forth the Wheel of Dhamma
translation_text.sn56.11:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana.
translation_text.sn56.11:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the group of five mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.11:2.1: “Mendicants, these two extremes should not be cultivated by one who has gone forth.
translation_text.sn56.11:2.3: Indulgence in sensual pleasures, which is low, crude, ordinary, ignoble, and pointless. And indulgence in self-mortification, which is painful, ignoble, and pointless.
translation_text.sn56.11:2.4: Avoiding these two extremes, the Realized One understood the middle way of practice, which gives vision and knowledge, and leads to peace, direct knowledge, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.sn56.11:3.1: And what is that middle way of practice?
translation_text.sn56.11:3.2: It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn56.11:3.3: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn56.11:3.4: This is that middle way of practice, which gives vision and knowledge, and leads to peace, direct knowledge, awakening, and extinguishment.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.1: Now this is the noble truth of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.2: Rebirth is suffering; old age is suffering; illness is suffering; death is suffering; being coupled with the disliked is suffering; separation from the liked is suffering; not getting what you wish for is suffering. In brief, the five grasping aggregates are suffering.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.3: Now this is the noble truth of the origin of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.4: It’s the craving that leads to future lives, mixed up with relishing and greed, taking pleasure wherever it lands. That is,
translation_text.sn56.11:4.5: craving for sensual pleasures, craving to continue existence, and craving for nonexistence.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.6: Now this is the noble truth of the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.7: It’s the fading away and cessation of that very same craving with nothing left over; giving it away, letting it go, releasing it, and not clinging to it.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.8: Now this is the noble truth of the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.11:4.9: It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.sn56.11:4.10: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn56.11:5.1: ‘This is the noble truth of suffering.’ Such was the vision, knowledge, wisdom, realization, and light that arose in me regarding teachings not learned before from another.
translation_text.sn56.11:5.2: ‘This noble truth of suffering should be completely understood.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:5.3: ‘This noble truth of suffering has been completely understood.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:6.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the origin of suffering.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:6.2: ‘This noble truth of the origin of suffering should be given up.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:6.3: ‘This noble truth of the origin of suffering has been given up.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:7.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the cessation of suffering.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:7.2: ‘This noble truth of the cessation of suffering should be realized.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:7.3: ‘This noble truth of the cessation of suffering has been realized.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:8.1: ‘This is the noble truth of the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:8.2: ‘This noble truth of the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering should be developed.’ Such was the vision that arose in me …
translation_text.sn56.11:8.3: ‘This noble truth of the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering has been developed.’ Such was the vision, knowledge, wisdom, realization, and light that arose in me regarding teachings not learned before from another.
translation_text.sn56.11:9.1: As long as my true knowledge and vision about these four noble truths was not fully purified in these three rounds and twelve aspects, I didn’t announce my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.sn56.11:10.1: But when my true knowledge and vision about these four noble truths was fully purified in these three rounds and twelve aspects, I announced my supreme perfect awakening in this world with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans.
translation_text.sn56.11:10.2: Knowledge and vision arose in me:
translation_text.sn56.11:10.3: ‘My freedom is unshakable; this is my last rebirth; now there’ll be no more future lives.’”
translation_text.sn56.11:10.4: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn56.11:10.5: Satisfied, the group of five mendicants approved what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn56.11:11.1: And while this discourse was being spoken, the stainless, immaculate vision of the Dhamma arose in Venerable Koṇḍañña:
translation_text.sn56.11:11.2: “Everything that has a beginning has an end.”
translation_text.sn56.11:12.1: And when the Buddha rolled forth the Wheel of Dhamma, the earth gods raised the cry:
translation_text.sn56.11:12.2: “Near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana, the Buddha has rolled forth the supreme Wheel of Dhamma. And that wheel cannot be rolled back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.”
translation_text.sn56.11:12.3: Hearing the cry of the earth gods, the gods of the four great kings …
translation_text.sn56.11:12.8: the gods who love to create …
translation_text.sn56.11:12.9: the gods who control what is created by others …
translation_text.sn56.11:12.10: the gods of the Divinity’s host raised the cry:
translation_text.sn56.11:12.11: “Near Varanasi, in the deer park at Isipatana, the Buddha has rolled forth the supreme Wheel of Dhamma. And that wheel cannot be rolled back by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.”
translation_text.sn56.11:13.1: And so at that moment, that second, that hour, the cry soared up to the realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn56.11:13.2: And this ten-thousandfold galaxy shook and rocked and trembled. And an immeasurable, magnificent light appeared in the world, surpassing the glory of the gods.
translation_text.sn56.11:14.1: Then the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.sn56.11:14.2: “Koṇḍañña has really understood! Koṇḍañña has really understood!”
translation_text.sn56.11:14.3: And that’s how Venerable Koṇḍañña came to be known as “Koṇḍañña Who Understood”.

variant_text.sn56.11:4.4: ponobbhavikā → ponobhavikā (bj) | seyyathidaṁ → seyyathīdaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.sn56.11:9.1: abhisambuddho’ti paccaññāsiṁ → abhisambuddho paccaññāsiṁ (bj, sya-all, km)
variant_text.sn56.11:13.1: tena layena → etthantare pāṭho si, sya-all, km potthakesu
